title: ariocarpus fissuratus subsp. lloydii
intro: Ariocarpus fissuratus subsp. lloydii, also known as the Lloyd's Peacock Pincushion, is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae and is native to Mexico. This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing due to its high concentration of mucilages and saponins. The Lloyd's Peacock Pincushion has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated tissues. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by boiling the stems in water or extracting the active compounds with a solvent like ethanol.
